description.task: Solve the Fractional Knapsack problem: given items with weights and values, and a knapsack capacity, maximize total value. Unlike 0/1 Knapsack, you may take fractions of items. Sort items by value/weight ratio and greedily fill.

You have tasks with deadlines and processing times. Schedule all tasks to minimize the maximum lateness (lateness = completion time - deadline, or 0 if early). Greedy: earliest deadline first minimizes max lateness.
instructions: Add the sort call at the start of minMaxLateness, before the loop
After sorting by deadline: C(d=3), B(d=4), A(d=6)
C: time=1, lateness=max(0,1-3)=0; B: time=3, lateness=max(0,3-4)=0; A: time=6, lateness=max(0,6-6)=0
description.expectedOutput:
--- Greedy (earliest deadline first) ---
  Task C: finish=1 deadline=3 lateness=0
  Task B: finish=3 deadline=4 lateness=0
  Task A: finish=6 deadline=6 lateness=0
Max lateness: 0

description.task: Given N gas stations in a circle with gas[i] available and cost[i] to travel to the next station, find the starting station index to complete a circular tour, or return -1 if no solution exists. Use O(n) greedy: track total and current surplus.
instructions: gas2={2,3,4}, cost2={3,4,3}: total net=2+3+4−3−4−3=−1 < 0 → impossible, return -1
Key insight: if a solution exists, it is unique and the greedy finds it in one pass
